channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Inside LBMX’s Workplace Culture and Commitment to Employee Growth
item.description: LBMX Inc., a London, Ontario-based technology company serving independent businesses in the building, HVAC and plumbing sectors, has built a robust workplace culture that continues to draw high-calibr...

item.title: MPs approve Sh25bn release to prevent affordable housing projects from stalling
item.description: Members of Parliament have stepped in to prevent a major slowdown in Kenya’s affordable housing projects by approving a Sh25 billion allocation under Supplementary Estimates I for the 2025/26 financia...

item.title: Inkaba Farming Project boosts local employment and agricultural growth in Mpumalanga
item.description: The Inkaba Farming Project, established by 36-year-old Mfanzile Maseko in Mooiplaas, Gert Sibande District Municipality, illustrates how agriculture can foster economic growth in rural South Africa. S...

item.title: Former Executive Assistant Sues Howard and Beth Stern Over Workplace Allegations
item.description: Howard Stern, the American radio host, and his wife Beth are facing a lawsuit from their former executive assistant, Leslie Kuhn, who claims the couple created a hostile work environment and misused n...

item.title: Albanese Embraces Labor Principles Amid Energy and Economic Challenges
item.description: Cameron Milner argues that after four years as Prime Minister, Anthony Albanese is beginning to reconnect with his Labor roots amid Australia’s worsening energy crisis. While his short national addres...

item.title: Emotional Stories from Families of Victims in Kariandusi Road Tragedy
item.description: The Kariandusi road accident in Nakuru County on April 3, 2026, left 11 people dead, including a nurse, Joseph Simiyu, whose wife, Nancy Barongo, shared an emotional account of their last conversation...

item.title: Traders Call for Urgent Action to Address Market Sanitation Crisis in Tanga
item.description: Over 1,700 traders at Mgandini Market in Tanga City have urged the city authorities, led by Mayor Mustafa Selebos, to urgently address the deteriorating sanitation conditions that are threatening publ...

item.title: Former Australian SAS Soldier Faces War Crime Charges for Killing Unarmed Afghans
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, Australia’s most decorated living veteran, has been charged with five counts of war crime murder related to his service in Afghanistan from 2009 to 2012. Roberts-Smith, a former cor...

item.title: Royal Mail raises stamp prices amid delivery performance concerns
item.description: Royal Mail has increased the price of its first class stamp to £1.80, a 10p rise, while second class letters now cost 91p, up 4p. This marks the eighth price increase in five years, driven by a declin...

item.title: Government increases daily LPG cylinder quota for migrant workers to improve access and supply
item.description: The Union Petroleum Ministry of India has issued a directive doubling the allocation of 5 kg free trade LPG cylinders for distribution to migrant workers in all States. The order, released late on Apr...

item.title: Finance Minister Confirms Gulf Remittances Stable Despite Middle East Tensions
item.description: Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb has assured that, so far, Pakistan’s remittances from Gulf countries have not been affected by the ongoing conflict triggered by the US-Israeli strikes on Iran. He ...

item.title: Lagos State GDP projected to reach N73.15 trillion in 2026, LEDU report shows
item.description: According to the latest Lagos Development Update (LEDU) 2026 report, Lagos State dey expect big growth for im economy. Dem project say nominal GDP go rise from N62.66 trillion wey e reach in 2025 to N...

item.title: Protesta ng PISTON at KMU laban sa taas-presyo ng langis
item.description: Ngayong araw, nagdaos ng isang protesta ang mga miyembro ng PISTON (Pinag-isang Samahan ng mga Tsuper at Operators Nationwide) at KMU (Kilusang Mayo Uno) laban sa patuloy na pagtaas ng presyo ng langi...

item.title: Escalating US-Israel-Iran Conflict and Regional Attacks
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have escalated sharply as US President Donald Trump set a Tuesday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, threatening to attack Iranian infrastructure if the dema...

item.title: ADB to Help Pakistan Strengthen Solar Energy and Power Sector Reforms
item.description: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) has expressed commitment to assist Pakistan in modernising its power sector through financial and technical support, particularly focusing on solar energy and battery ...

item.title: Minister Katambi Warns Citizens to Stop Spreading False Genital Theft Claims
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The Tanzanian Minister for Home Affairs, Mr. Patrobas Katambi, has issued a serious warning to all citizens against spreading rumours that their private parts have been stolen. He stat...

item.title: Oga Obinna Clarifies Karen Nyamu Attended Vurugu Fight Without Paying for Ticket
item.description: Kenyan YouTuber Oga Obinna has stirred conversation after disclosing that nominated senator Karen Nyamu attended his much-hyped Vurugu Fight Night without supporting the event financially. The boxing ...

item.title: Madlanga Commission recommences hearings focusing on TMPD allegations
item.description: The Madlanga Commission, chaired by retired Constitutional Court Justice Mbuyiseli Madlanga, has resumed its public hearings following the Easter break. This week, the Commission will examine allegati...

item.title: Iranian Agent Claims Pitso Mosimane’s Wife Owes R1.2 Million Commission in Esteghlal Coaching Deal
item.description: Former Mamelodi Sundowns coach Pitso Mosimane and his wife Moira Tlhagale are reportedly embroiled in a R1.2 million legal dispute with Iranian football agent Hamed Ghadiri. According to the report, G...

item.title: Coping With Grief Through Unexpected Emotional Responses After a Partner's Suicide
item.description: Red Hoffman recounts the complex emotional journey following the suicide of her partner, B. Despite years of dealing with infidelity and acting as B's caregiver after a traumatic brain injury, Hoffman...
